perturbation.original_text: 19. The validity and interpretation of this Agreement will be governed by, and construed and enforced in accordance with, the laws of the State of New York, without giving effect to conflicts of laws principles. You irrevocably (i) submit to the jurisdiction of any court of the State of New York located in New York, New York or the United States District Court for the Southern District of New York for the purpose of any suit, action, or other proceeding arising out of this Agreement, or any of the transactions contemplated hereby (each a “Proceeding”) and agree that service of any process, summons, notice or document delivered by hand or sent by U.S. registered mail to your address set forth above will be effective service of process for any action, suit or proceeding brought against you in any such court, (ii) agree that all claims in respect of any Proceeding may be heard and determined in any such court, (iii) irrevocably waive, to the fullest extent permitted by law, any immunity you have acquired, or hereafter may acquire, from jurisdiction of any such court or from any legal process therein, (iv) irrevocably waive, to the fullest extent permitted by law, any right to trial by jury in any Proceeding and (v) agree not to commence any Proceeding other than in such court, and waive, to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, any claim that any such Proceeding is brought in an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.changed_text:
17. You represent and warrant that you (i) are acting as a principal in any possible Transaction, (ii) are not represented by any broker or similar party other than Wells Fargo Securities, LLC, which company is acting as your financial advisor in connection with a possible Transaction, (iii) are not acting as a broker for or Representative of any other person in connection with the Transaction, and (iv) are considering the Transaction only for your own account. Except with the prior written consent of the Company, you agree that (a) you will not act as a joint bidder or co-bidder with any other person with respect to the Transaction, and (b) neither you nor any of your Representatives (acting on behalf of you or your subsidiaries) will enter into any discussions, negotiations, agreements, arrangements or understandings (whether written or oral) with any other person regarding the Transaction, other than the Company and its Representatives, and your Representatives (to the extent permitted hereunder).

19. The validity and interpretation of this Agreement will be governed by, and construed and enforced in accordance with, the laws of the State of Delaware, without giving effect to conflicts of laws principles. You irrevocably (i) submit to the jurisdiction of any court of the State of Delaware located in New Castle County or the United States District Court for the District of Delaware for the purpose of any suit, action, or other proceeding arising out of this Agreement, or any of the transactions contemplated hereby (each a “Proceeding”) and agree that service of any process, summons, notice or document delivered by hand or sent by U.S. registered mail to your address set forth above will be effective service of process for any action, suit or proceeding brought against you in any such court, (ii) agree that all claims in respect of any Proceeding may be heard and determined in any such court, (iii) irrevocably waive, to the fullest extent permitted by law, any immunity you have acquired, or hereafter may acquire, from jurisdiction of any such court or from any legal process therein, (iv) irrevocably waive, to the fullest extent permitted by law, any right to trial by jury in any Proceeding and (v) agree not to commence any Proceeding other than in such court, and waive, to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, any claim that any such Proceeding is brought in an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.explanation: The governing law and jurisdiction clause (originally paragraph 19) has been relocated to paragraph 17 and the governing law has been changed to Delaware. Governing law clauses are intended to be grouped together to avoid confusion.
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification attempts to alter the governing law and venue, which may contradict the expectations of parties and the original intent. 28 U.S. Code § 1391 establishes rules for determining proper venue in federal courts, which is implicitly related to the governing law chosen in a contract. Changing the governing law could affect the venue analysis, especially if the new jurisdiction has different rules or standards for determining where a lawsuit can be brought. By placing this clause in an unusual location, it increases the likelihood of oversight and potential disputes regarding venue.

perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that the governing law was changed to Delaware and relocated within the contract. The Law Explanation states this could contradict the original intent of the parties. 28 U.S. Code § 1391, as stated in Scraped Law Snippet 2, establishes rules for venue in federal courts, implicitly related to the governing law. The Changed Text moves the clause and changes the state, impacting venue analysis, potentially contradicting the original agreement's intent and increasing the risk of disputes due to the unusual placement, creating a Structural Flaw Legal Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Until the date that is eighteen (18) months from the date of this Agreement, you will not, without the Company’s prior written consent, directly or indirectly solicit for purposes of employment, offer to hire or engage as a consultant, entice away or offer to enter into any contract with, or hire or engage as a consultant or enter into any contract with, during the period of employment or the 90-day period following such employment, any senior or key employee of the Company or its controlled affiliates who becomes known to you in connection with your evaluation of the Transaction; provided , however , that this paragraph will not prohibit you or any of your Representatives from engaging in any general advertising or general solicitation not targeted to any employees or former employees of the Company or its controlled affiliates, or from hiring any employees or former employees of the Company who respond to such solicitation. Finally, this paragraph 7 will not apply to any solicitation in the ordinary course of business initiated by one of your employees who has no knowledge of this Agreement or a potential Transaction, so long as such individual is acting without information or encouragement from any of your Representatives who does possess such knowledge.
perturbation.changed_text:
24. The terms of this Agreement shall supersede any additional purported confidentiality requirements imposed by any offering memorandum, web-based database or similar repository of Evaluation Material to which you or any of your Representatives may be granted access in connection with the evaluation, negotiation or consummation of the Transaction, notwithstanding acceptance of such an offering memorandum or submission of an electronic signature, “clicking” on an “I Agree” icon or other indication of assent to such additional confidentiality conditions, it being understood and agreed that your confidentiality obligations with respect to Evaluation Material are exclusively governed by this Agreement and may not be enlarged except by a written agreement that is hereafter executed by each of the parties hereto.

7. Until the date that is eighteen (18) months from the date of this Agreement, you will not, without the Company’s prior written consent, directly or indirectly solicit for purposes of employment, offer to hire or engage as a consultant, entice away or offer to enter into any contract with, or hire or engage as a consultant or enter into any contract with, during the period of employment or the 90-day period following such employment, any employee of the Company or its controlled affiliates who becomes known to you. Further, this paragraph 7 will apply to any solicitation in the ordinary course of business initiated by one of your employees, even if such employee has no knowledge of this Agreement or a potential Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: The non-solicitation clause has been altered to remove exceptions, making it more strict. This modification could affect enforceability because courts often look at the reasonableness of non-solicitation agreements. Moving this clause to a general section reduces its visibility and increases the chance it will be overlooked.
perturbation.law_explanation: The added strictness and removal of the exception of the original text may violate the Restatement (Second) of Contracts § 188, which dictates the reasonableness of restraint of trade. This can potentially make the section void because it overly limits the scope of potential employees one may solicit, and may be unnecessarily restrictive.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-302. Unconscionable contract or Clause. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-302. Unconscionable contract or Clause. (1) If the court as a matter of law finds the contract or any clause of the contract to have been unconscionable at the time it was made the court may refuse to enforce the contract, or it may enforce the remainder of the contract without the unconscionable clause, or it may so limit the application of any unconscionable clause as to avoid any unconscionable result. (2) When it is claimed or appears to the court that the contract or any clause thereof may be unconscionable the parties shall be afforded a reasonable opportunity to present evidence as to its commercial setting, purpose and effect to aid the court in making the determination. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Unconscionable - Definition, Examples, Cases, Processes Unconscionable August 30, 2017 by: Content Team Courts use the term “unconscionable” to describe conduct that is either unjust or one-sided to benefit one party more than the other. In contract law , an unconscionable contract is one that no reasonable person would enter into without some very compelling reason. Those who do end up entering into unconscionable contracts tend to be poorly educated, living in poverty, and/or have been unable to find a fairer agreement elsewhere. To explore this concept, consider the following unconscionable definition. Definition of Unconscionable Noun Conduct that is unfair or one-sided so as to provide more of a benefit to one party over another. Origin 1555-1565 What is Unconscionability Unconscionability is a term in contract law that is used to describe the terms of a contract that are so severely unfair or one-sided that no reasonable person would enter into such a contract. Because of how unreasonable an unconscionable contract is, the contract is considered by law to be unenforceable. This is because it would not be fair for the person who drafted the contract to benefit from his deliberate deception. It would also be equally unfair not to let the other party out of a contract that was drafted for the purpose of deceiving him. For example, unconscionable language consists of terms or phrases that the average person would not understand. This lack of understanding would then be glossed over by the perpetrator in his attempt to have the other party sign a contract that he would not have agreed to, had he more fully understood what he was signing. A contract of sale to purchase a home wherein the real estate agent inserted hidden fees or other shady terms that are written in “legalese” would be an example of unconscionable conduct. Without seeking the assistance of an attorney, the average person may not understand what he is agreeing to, but may agree to it anyway due to a lack of education on the subject, or the belief that he will not find a better deal elsewhere. Unconscionable Conduct Unconscionable conduct is typically found in cases that involve fraud or deception. This is because one party deliberately misrepresents the facts to deprive someone else of something valuable, such as money or property. In effect, the person exhibiting the unconscionable conduct is stealing from, or otherwise taking advantage of, the other person. Unconscionable conduct can be punished as either a criminal fraud, or with a civil action. A contract is more likely to be found to be unconscionable if it contains both unfair bargaining practices and one-sided terms. Unconscionable Contract A contract is considered unconscionable when the person drafting the contract was acting with unconscionable conduct at the time the contract was being drafted. The fact that events may transpire later on that provide one party with more of an extreme benefit than the other is irrelevant, and do not make for an unconscionable contract. Unconscionability must exist at the time the contract is drafted in order for the contract to be declared void, and for the drafter to be punished accordingly. There are no set guidelines insofar as determining whether a contract is unconscionable. It is left up to the discretion of the judge or jury that is presiding over the case. An unconscionable contract is one that would be a mockery of the judicial system if the court were to let it stand. Damages are not typically awarded upon a court’s finding of a contract to be unconscionable. However, there are other remedies available at law that a court can pursue. For instance, the court may refuse to enforce an unconscionable contract, arguing that the party who was unfairly treated was misled, lacked the information necessary to make such a decision, or signed under duress . Alternatively, the court may refuse to enforce only the clause that makes the contract unconscionable and uphold
perturbation.justification: The modification removes exceptions from the non-solicitation clause, potentially making it overly broad and unreasonable. The Law Explanation suggests this could violate the Restatement (Second) of Contracts § 188 concerning reasonableness of restraint of trade. While Restatement (Second) of Contracts § 188 is not among the scraped snippets, Scraped Law Snippet 2 defines "unconscionable" as "unfair or one-sided so as to provide more of a benefit to one party over another," which is relevant if the non-solicitation clause is deemed too restrictive. This increases the likelihood that a court would find the clause unenforceable because it excessively limits potential employment opportunities for former employees, contradicting the principle of fair contractual terms.

perturbation.original_text: 13. You acknowledge that certain of the Evaluation Material may be subject to export restrictions under U.S. law. You will not, and will cause your Representatives not to, without the express written permission of the Company, transmit, directly or indirectly, any Evaluation Material that is subject to such export restrictions to any person or country outside the United States or otherwise in violation of applicable law; provided , however , that you will not be deemed to be in violation of this provision in connection with any Evaluation Material provided directly by the Company or its Representatives to your employees who have a Randstad email address other than one ending in “randstadusa.com”. The Company will advise you in writing as to the applicability of this Section 13 to any particular piece of Evaluation Material at or before the time such material is provided to you.
perturbation.changed_text:
3. Unless otherwise required by applicable law or legal process or stock exchange rule or regulation, but only after compliance with paragraph 6 below under the heading “Compelled Disclosure,” neither the Company nor its Representatives will, without your prior written consent, disclose to any other person that investigations, discussions or negotiations have taken or may take place concerning a possible Transaction, or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such possible Transaction, including, without limitation, the status thereof, the existence and terms of this Agreement and the fact that the Evaluation Material has been made available to you.

13. You acknowledge that certain of the Evaluation Material may be subject to import restrictions under U.S. law. You will not, and will cause your Representatives not to, without the express written permission of the Company, transmit, directly or indirectly, any Evaluation Material that is subject to such import restrictions to any person or country outside the United States or otherwise in violation of applicable law. The Company will not advise you in writing as to the applicability of this Section 13 to any particular piece of Evaluation Material at or before the time such material is provided to you.
perturbation.explanation: The export restrictions clause is modified to reference 'import restrictions' instead, and remove the advisory notice from the company. Relocating this to a standard confidentiality section obscures it, increasing the risk that the recipient won't be aware of US export/import control requirements. The original text has 'export restrictions' which is related to compliance with EAR and ITAR.
perturbation.law_explanation: By changing the terms and location, the contract fails to adequately inform the recipient of US export regulations requirements under the EAR (15 CFR Parts 730-774). The EAR requires that parties involved in the export of certain items be aware of and comply with licensing requirements and other restrictions. The advisory notice removal makes the other party responsible for understanding these regulations on their own, potentially leading to non-compliance.

Skip to main content Export Administration Regulations (EAR) This tool helps you navigate the Export Administration Regulations (EAR) under 15 CFR 730-774. Commerce Control List (CCL) The Commerce Control List (CCL) (Supplement No. 1 to Part 774 of the EAR) is divided into ten broad categories, and each category is further subdivided into five product groups. Additionally, you may explore our interactive tool . Category 0 - Nuclear Materials Facilities & Equipment [and Miscellaneous Items] Category 1 - Special Materials and Related Equipment, Chemicals, “Microorganisms,” and “Toxins” Category 2 - Materials Processing Category 3 - Electronics Category 4 - Computers Category 5 Part 1 - Telecommunications Category 5 Part 2 - Information Security Category 6 - Sensors and Lasers Category 7 - Navigation and Avionics Category 8 - Marine Category 9 - Aerospace and Propulsion Legal limitations of the tools on the BIS website The tools on the BIS website are not intended to replace the official rules found in the Code of Federal Regulations , nor are they a primer on how to get started. For that introductory guidance, please visit our Learn and Support page. The tools are an editorial compilation of material and not an official legal edition of the Code of Federal Regulations or the Federal Register . We have made every effort to ensure the material presented in these tools are accurate, but if you are relying on them for legal research you should consult the official editions of those sources to confirm your findings. Nothing in these tools binds BIS or creates any rights, benefits, or defenses, substantive or procedural, that are enforceable by any party in any manner. If you find any errors, please email us.

perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens compliance with US export regulations by changing 'export restrictions' to 'import restrictions' and removing the advisory notice, potentially leading to non-compliance. The original text clearly acknowledges export restrictions under U.S. law and includes an advisory notice. The modified text, by removing this notice and relocating the altered text to a confidentiality section, obscures the importance of export compliance as detailed in the Export Administration Regulations (EAR). According to Scraped Law Snippet 1, 'This tool helps you navigate the Export Administration Regulations (EAR) under 15 CFR 730-774'. By obscuring this requirement, the contract increases the risk of non-compliance. The structural flaw of placing this altered clause in a confidentiality section further exacerbates the issue, making it less likely that the recipient will be adequately informed of their responsibilities.

perturbation.changed_text:
14. This Agreement defines the duties and obligations of you and your Representatives with respect to the Evaluation Material to the extent it may be disclosed or made available but does not constitute or create any obligation of the Company or its Representatives to provide any Evaluation Material or other information to you. Under no circumstances will the Company or any of its Representatives be obligated to disclose or make available any information, including, without limitation, any Evaluation Material, which the Company in its sole and absolute discretion determines not to disclose. Except for any express representations and warranties set forth in a definitive agreement providing for a Transaction and subject to the terms, conditions and limitations of any such agreement, you understand and acknowledge that none of the Company or any of their respective Representatives is making any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material or any other information provided to you or your Representatives by or on behalf of the Company, and none of the Company or any of their respective Representatives will have any liability to you or any other person (including, without limitation, any of your Representatives) resulting from your use of the Evaluation Material or any errors therein or omissions therefrom, except as may otherwise be set forth in any definitive agreement providing for a Transaction and subject to the terms, conditions and limitations of any such agreement.

Without the prior written consent of the Company, you will not, directly or indirectly, engage in discussions regarding equity financing of a possible Transaction with any person, or otherwise engage in any discussions regarding a possible Transaction or enter into in any agreement, arrangement or understanding (or any discussions which might lead to an agreement, arrangement or understanding) with any person regarding participation in a possible Transaction as a principal, co-investor or source of equity financing. perturbation.explanation: The clause about "Representatives" is modified to restrict debt financing sources from being included. This could violate good faith dealing by hindering the recipient's ability to obtain necessary financing, particularly since the debt financing sources are required to abide to the same confidentiality constraints.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Uniform Commercial Code § 1-304 - Obligation of Good Faith
perturbation.law_explanation: This change may constitute a breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. UCC § 1-304 states every contract or duty within the UCC imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement. By restricting the ability of Randstad to work with financing sources, it would be considered a violation because it impedes Randstad from obtaining financing for this transaction.

perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the definition of "Representatives" to exclude potential debt financing sources, which the Perturbation Explanation suggests could violate good faith dealing. The Law Explanation and Scraped Law Snippet 1 indicate that UCC § 1-304 imposes an obligation of good faith in the performance and enforcement of every contract. By restricting the recipient's ability to work with financing sources, the modified text arguably impedes their ability to obtain financing for the transaction, therefore it could be considered a violation of good faith, thus a legal contradiction.
